remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Spontaneous elite control (natural viremic suppression without ART) in <1% of people living with HIV; untreated, median time to AIDS ~10 years; viral rebound within weeks of ART interruption in 99%+ of patients
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Modern ART (integrase inhibitor-based): viral suppression to undetectable in >95% within 6 months, maintained indefinitely on treatment; life expectancy near-normalised with early treatment; 7 documented sterilising cures worldwide (all via CCR5-Δ32 stem cell transplantation); post-treatment controllers (VISCONTI cohort): ~5-15% of early-treated individuals achieve durable ART-free viremic suppression
remission.gap_size: Very large (sterilising cure); near-zero biological gap for quality-of-life control on ART; large access gap globally
remission.barrier_type: biological (sterilising cure) + access (ART, particularly in LMIC)
remission.barrier_detail: ART is the most successful chronic disease management story in medicine but is definitionally not a cure; 39 million people living with HIV globally, only 77% on ART, only 72% virally suppressed (2023 UNAIDS); broadly neutralising antibodies (bNAbs) showing promising functional remission signals (ACTG A5386, 2025)
remission.last_soc_change: 2022-2023 — long-acting injectables (cabotegravir + rilpivirine every 2 months, lenacapavir every 6 months) transformed adherence paradigm; dolutegravir-based regimens as first-line since 2018; ART inception for all regardless of CD4 count since 2015 (START trial); first drug (AZT) 1987
remission.outdated_treatments: Older NRTIs (stavudine, didanosine) still used in some LMIC settings despite lipoatrophy and neuropathy; CD4-gated treatment initiation (replaced by treat-all since 2015); unboosted PI regimens (higher pill burden, metabolic effects vs INSTIs)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Long-acting PrEP (lenacapavir 6-monthly injection, PURPOSE 1/2 trials: 100% efficacy in PURPOSE 1 for adolescent girls); broadly neutralising antibodies (bNAbs) VRC01, 3BNC117 for functional remission; latency-reversing agents combined with immune checkpoint blockade
remission.frontier_signal: bNAb combinations (ACTG A5386 — functional remission signal 2025); lenacapavir + bNAb for HIV cure; gene editing (zinc finger nucleases, CRISPR targeting CCR5 and HIV reservoir — EBT-101 Phase 1/2); stem cell-derived CCR5-Δ32 gene editing (City of Hope trial)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
